title: SACRAMENTO

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Affordable Housing Bond Act of 2026 aims to provide $10 billion for California's affordable housing programs.",
    "The bond measure is expected to result in more than 35,000 new homes affordable to very-low income, extremely-low income, and homeless families.",
    "The Statewide Housing Plan estimates that California needs approximately 300,000 new housing units annually.",
    "California has produced approximately 13,000 affordable units per year from 2014-2023.",
    "Only 17 percent of households in California can afford to purchase the median-priced single-family home.",
    "More than half of renters and 65 percent of low-income renters in California are rent-burdened, paying more than 30 percent of their income toward housing.",
    "In 2024, over 187,000 Californians experienced homelessness on a given night.",
    "Funds from California's last housing bond, the Veterans and Affordable Housing Bond Act of 2018, were fully allocated by the end of 2024.",
    "Every $1 invested by the state in the housing bond is expected to be matched by $4 in additional funding sources.",
    "California needs to build more affordable homes to tackle its housing crisis."
  ]
}
```
